channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Discord accused of exposing kids to graphic, violent content in lawsuit
item.description: The complaint, filed in New Jersey Superior Court, alleges Discord was aware of its safety features and policies but did not take action to protect young users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: TSA tests out AI to train, assist airport screening officers
item.description: TSA is testing the use of AI and virtual reality to help assist its tens of thousands of airport screeners The post TSA tests out AI to train, assist airport screening officers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Samsung's latest smartphone has a very simple feature that no other Samsung phone offers right now
item.description: Samsung brings back the removable battery with the rugged Galaxy XCover7 Pro, offering reliability and endurance in challenging work environments.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Dell data center modernization gear targets AI, HPC workloads
item.description: Dell Technology has introduced a raft of new products aimed at advancing customers’ data center modernization projects capable of supporting a variety of workloads, from virtualization and database management to AI inferencing and high-performance computing (HPC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google previews Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model
item.description: Google has introduced an early preview of its Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model. An early version of the model is available through the Gemini API via Google AI Studio and Vertex AI, according to an April 17 Google blog post.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders will start in the United States on April 24, and the price is not increasing
item.description: Nintendo has finally announced that Switch 2 preorders will start in the United States on April 24, 2025 with no price increase for the console.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — America’s only rare earths mine heard from anxious companies soon after China responded to President Donald Trump’s tariffs this month by limiting exports of those minerals used for military applications and in many high-tech devices.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 8 ways diversity and inclusion help teams perform better
item.description: Diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives have been taking a beating lately. On his first day in office, US President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling government efforts to advance racial equity and to support underserved communities “illegal and immoral discrimination.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tariffs on PCs: What You Need to Know Before Buying
item.description: Tariffs on PCs: What You Need to Know Before Buying
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The project of reform and revival that saved JAL
item.description: A major turning point came for JAL in 2010 when the airline filed for bankruptcy and subsequently announced a proposed rehabilitation plan that included a much needed IT system renewal. Since 1967, JAL used the JALCOM reservation and ticketing system, built using Assembler on an IBM mainframe.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How U.S. tariffs could impact cloud computing
item.description: The cloud computing industry has long been the backbone of digital transformation. In 2022 alone, this sector surpassed $480 billion in global valuation, and its growth trajectory remains strong.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google’s digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Facebook Groups are fueling a black market for Uber and DoorDash accounts, says a new report
item.description: A new watchdog report uncovers Facebook groups quietly fueling a black market for operational Uber and DoorDash accounts, posing serious safety concerns for riders and customers.
item.dc:creator: Arden Anderson, Fast Company

item.title: Lawmakers launch investigation into 23andMe after bankruptcy
item.description: A group of House lawmakers launched a probe Thursday into 23andMe's handling of customers' sensitive data following the company's filing for bankruptcy last month. In a letter to 23andMe, House Commerce and Energy Committee Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) and Reps. Gus Bilirakis (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Broadcom (quietly) brings back free VMware ESXi hypervisor
item.description: With little notice or fanfare, Broadcom is offering a free version of the VMware ESXi hypervisor for download. VMware vSphere Hypervisor version 8 can be downloaded from Broadcom’s support portal for free, but registration is required.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: San Jose mayor Mahan, local congressman Liccardo lend support as Waymo applies to bring robotaxis to the city
item.description: Waymo initially faced a rocky road in bringing its pioneering technology to Bay Area streets and highways.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: CoreWeave offers cloud-based Grace Blackwell GPUs for AI training
item.description: Cloud services provider CoreWeave has announced it is offering Nvidia’s GB200 NVL72 systems, otherwise known as “Grace Blackwell,” to customers looking to do intensive AI training.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Insta360 teaser suggests it could launch world's best 360 camera soon – here are 5 things I want to see
item.description: Insta360 has posted a teaser for a new launch event on April 22, and rumors are pointing towards a new X5. Here's what we want to see from the 360 camera.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Endangered sea turtle populations show signs of recovery in more than half the world, survey finds
item.description: A new global survey finds that endangered sea turtles show signs of recovery in a majority of places where they’re found worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thankfully Mario Kart World will have some open-world challenges in Free Roam mode
item.description: Nintendo has confirmed that Mario Kart World's Free Roam mode will feature challenges and collectibles.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Smartphones may boost children's mental health, study finds
item.description: The study's findings challenge the assumption that kids who own smartphones tend to fare worse than those who don't.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Nvidia announces plans to make AI supercomputers in US
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The network blueprint to take your modern enterprise global
item.description: Adopting new technologies is synonymous with improving productivity, enhancing decision-making, and driving innovations today. Almost every enterprise integrates some form of AI/ML capabilities within their operations or tapping into the Internet of Things (IoT) to keep operations at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Keysight tools tackle data center deployment efficiency
item.description: Test and performance measurement vendor Keysight Technologies has developed Keysight Artificial Intelligence (KAI) to identify performance inhibitors affecting large GPU deployments. It emulates workload profiles, rather than using actual resources, to pinpoint performance bottlenecks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, Fox 5
